input.background: In the cholesterol illustrative example, we aim to determine the minimal detectable difference using a sample size of n=30 per group, with a significance level of α=0.05 (two-sided) and a power of 0.90. A reasonable estimate for σ² has been established as 0.25.
input.question: What is the minimal detectable difference?

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: Δ=√(2⋅σ²/n)(z₁₋β+z₁₋α/₂)=√(2⋅0.25/30)(1.282+1.96)=0.42. This study will be able to detect a mean difference of 0.42 mmol/L under the stated conditions.

sample_size_per_group.description: The number of samples in each group.

significance_level.description: The significance level (alpha) for the hypothesis test, two-sided.

power.description: The power of the test, representing the probability of correctly rejecting the null hypothesis.

variance_estimate.description: The estimated variance (σ²) used for calculations.
